title: Setting Up 2FA
content:
Why 2FA Matters

Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) is one of the most effective ways to protect an account from unauthorized access. Even if a password is stolen or guessed, 2FA prevents login unless the attacker also has access to the user's authenticator device. Tklesson supports TOTP-based 2FA, which works with any standard authenticator app. Agents should recommend 2FA to every client — especially after a password reset or 2FA disable.

How to Guide a Client Through Setup

Clients may need help setting up 2FA for the first time, or re-enrolling after a disable. Walk them through these steps:

  1. Have the client log in to their Tklesson dashboard.
  2. Direct them to Settings or Account Security.
  3. Under Two-Factor Authentication, they click Enable.
  4. A QR code appears. The client opens their authenticator app and selects Add Account or the + icon.
  5. They scan the QR code. If scanning fails, they can manually enter the secret key displayed below the QR code.
  6. The app generates a 6-digit code that refreshes every 30 seconds. The client enters this code and clicks Verify.
  7. Once verified, recovery codes are displayed. Emphasize that these must be saved immediately.

Recommending an Authenticator App

If the client does not have an authenticator app, suggest one of these:

  • Google Authenticator — Free, simple, available on iOS and Android. No cloud backup — if the phone is lost, recovery codes are the only fallback.
  • Authy — Supports encrypted cloud backups and multi-device sync. Recommended for clients who want protection against device loss.
  • Microsoft Authenticator — Good option for clients already in the Microsoft ecosystem.

Recovery Codes — What to Tell the Client

Recovery codes are the only way back in if the authenticator device is lost. Make sure the client understands:

  • Each code can only be used once.
  • Codes should be stored somewhere separate from the phone — a password manager, a printed copy, or a secure note.
  • If all codes are used, the client must generate a new set from their security settings.
  • If both the device and recovery codes are lost, they will need to contact support and go through identity verification to have 2FA disabled.

Key Talking Points for Agents

  • "2FA protects your account even if someone gets your password."
  • "The setup takes less than two minutes."
  • "Save your recovery codes somewhere safe — they're your backup key."
  • "Tklesson will never ask for your 2FA code. If anyone does, it's a scam."
  • If the client changes phones: advise them to disable 2FA on the old device first, then re-enable on the new one — or use Authy with cloud backup enabled.

After Setup Is Complete

Confirm the client can log out and log back in successfully with their 2FA code. Remind them that each login will now require both their password and the code from their authenticator app. If they ever get locked out, our team can help after identity verification.
